sutta: monks there are these 5 losses what 5 loss of kin loss of wealth lost by disease loss of virtue and loss of view or right view monks not caused by loss of kin wealth or by disease do beings on the breaking up of the body after death arise in the wayward way the ill way the abyss hell monks caused by loss of virtue or by loss of view do beings on the breaking up of the body after death arise in the wayward way the ill way the abyss hell verily monks these are the 5 monks there are these 5 prophets what 5 prophet of kin prophet of wealth prophet of health prophet of virtue and prophet of view monks not caused by prophet of kin wealth or health do beings on the breaking up of the body after death arise in the happy way the heaven world monks caused by prophet of virtue or by prophet of view do beings after death arise in the happy way the heaven will apparently amongst these other 5 these this uttar says that a person goes to hell because of loss of virtue and loss of right view that means he has wrong view and he does not keep the precepts and the person goes to heaven because of virtue and because of right view the other things are loss of relatives or loss of wealth loss of health and that is not so important as virtue and right view that is why studying the dharma is so important because it gives us right view and from right view we will naturally keep the precepts and attain virtue or moral conduct so always remember right view is very very important number 5 point rolls on indeed no unroyal wheel and when he has thus spoken a certain monk said to the exalted 1 but who lord is the rajya of the raja the roller of the wheel the dhamma man the dhamma raja [Music] it is dhamma mang said the exalted 1 here in mung the raja the wheel roller the dhamma man the dhamma raja relies just on dhamma honours dhamma reveres dhamma esteems dharma with dhamma as his standard with dhamma as his banner with dhamma as his men did he sets a dharma watch and bar and ward for folk within his realm he sets a dharma watch and bar and ward for warrior and karen follower for brahmin and for householder for town and country for for recluse and for brahmin for beast and bird alike thus indeed among that raja setting a dhamma watch and bar and ward for folk and creatures within his realm rolls on the wheel by dhamma and that wheel may not be rolled back by the hand of any hostile creature even so among the tata relies just on dhamma honours dhamma reveres dhamma esteems dharma with dhamma as his standard with dharma as his banner with dharma as his mandate he sets a dharma watch and bar and word for monk saying follow you such a practice indeed not that other for you follow you such a practice in word not that other follow you such a practice in thought not that other follow you such a livelihood not that other see you such a town or village not that other so too for nuns and lay disciples both men and women thus indeed among that tata gather setting a dhamma watch and bar and for monk and none for lay disciples both men and women rose on by dhamma the unsurpassed wheel of dharma and that wheel may not be rolled back by recluse brahmin deva mara brahma or by any in the world that's the end of the sutta
commentary: here the buddha is saying that the buddha the dhamma king reveres dhamma as dhamma as his rajah so even the buddha has raja as a king which is the dhamma he honors dhamma he reveres dhamma he acts according to dhamma remember the other day we read mansuta how the buddha said because of respect for dhamma whenever he teaches dharma he is takes a lot of care in teaching the dhamma so too for us we must always honor dhamma and live our lives according to the dhamma [Music] the next suta
